article.title: Factors of Risk in the Development of Coronary Heart Disease - Six-Year Follow-up Experience
article.authors: Kannel WB, Dawber TR, Kagan A, Revotskie N, Stokes J III

study_type.notes: Prospective cohort study. Classic Framingham Heart Study. 5,127 participants free of CHD at baseline followed for 6 years. Multiple CHD risk factors (cholesterol, hypertension, ECG) evaluated.

1.notes: Title clearly indicates 'Six-Year Follow-up Experience The Framingham Study' - prospective cohort design

2.notes: Background: 'Cardiovascular disease is now the leading cause of death... prevention program is clearly necessary'

3.notes: Objective: 'study the characteristics of persons who develop coronary heart disease under observation...determine the characteristics of susceptible individuals'

4.notes: Study design clearly presented in Methods: prospective cohort design with baseline and follow-up examinations

5.notes: Setting: Framingham, Massachusetts. Study established 1948-1950. Follow-up period: 6 years

6a.notes: Eligibility: 6,507 persons selected at random, 4,469 examined and found free of CHD at baseline (ages 30-59), plus 740 volunteers. 5,127 total free of disease at entry

7.notes: Outcomes clearly defined: myocardial infarction, angina pectoris, sudden death. Risk factors: serum cholesterol, blood pressure, ECG patterns

8.notes: Data collection methods: 'Detailed history and physical examination by physician...Laboratory studies included chest X ray, electrocardiogram...blood glucose, uric acid, lipoproteins, cholesterol'

9.notes: No explicit discussion of potential biases or efforts to address bias

10.notes: No sample size calculation or power analysis described

11.notes: Quantitative variables (cholesterol, BP) presented but no specific discussion of categorization methods or cutoffs

12a.notes: Statistical methods described: incidence rates per 1,000 population, age-specific rates, comparison of means with significance testing

12b.notes: Subgroup analysis by sex and age group throughout results

12c.notes: No discussion of missing data handling

12d.notes: Follow-up accounting: '87% at clinic on fourth biennial examination...2.4% known to have died before their fourth examination'

13a.notes: Participant flow: Table 1 shows 6,507 selected, 4,469 examined, 4,393 free of CHD at baseline plus 734 volunteers = 5,127 total

13b.notes: Non-participation: 'Of the 6,507 persons selected at random, 4,469 persons...came in for examination' (implicit reasons: non-response/refusal)

14a.notes: Baseline characteristics: Tables 1, 5, 7 show age, sex, cholesterol levels, blood pressure distribution

14b.notes: No explicit reporting of completeness or missing data

15.notes: Outcome events: Tables 3A, 3B, 4 report incidence of CHD by type and sex. Overall 186 new cases in 6 years

16a.notes: Unadjusted estimates: incidence rates per 1,000 by cholesterol level, age, sex. Examples: '245 or more mg cholesterol: 120.3 per 1,000 in men vs 43.5 in women'

16b.notes: Category boundaries for cholesterol: '<210', '210-244', '245 or more' mg/100 ml. Age groups: 30-34, 35-39, etc.

16c.notes: Absolute incidence rates provided but not translated to number needed to treat

17.notes: Only basic stratified analysis by age and sex; no formal sensitivity analyses

18.notes: Key results: 'six-year incidence of 36.3 per thousand...in men was 54.8 per thousand...in women 21.4 per thousand'

19.notes: No discussion of study limitations or sources of bias

20.notes: No cautious interpretation or discussion of alternative explanations

21.notes: No discussion of generalizability or external validity

22.notes: Funding acknowledged: 'From the Heart Disease Epidemiology Study, Framingham, Mass., and the National Heart Institute'
